libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. If tests exercise Persistence bootstrap APIs, install a test PersistenceProviderResolver via PersistenceProviderResolverHolder and restore it afterward; do not add Hibernate/EclipseLink or a database just to make bootstrap calls work. Update any Query/TypedQuery stub implementations for the 4.0.0-M5 API surface, including setParameters(Object...).

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8596 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.persistence:jakarta.persistence-api:4.0.0-M5; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact is a specification/API jar, so provider-specific behavior is outside scope unless a concrete provider is intentionally added by the test author.
Milestone 4.0.0-M5 adds or changes abstract API methods, so hand-written stubs can fail javac if they are not kept in sync.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No deterministic setup is needed: jakarta.persistence-api is an API-only artifact with only the transitive jakarta.annotation-api dependency, and meaningful coverage can be reached with annotations, SPI/API stubs, and PersistenceProviderResolver test doubles rather than a real provider, database, Docker image, or environment setting.
